creator: remko@elvandar.org
text:
On Tue, Jan 16, 2007 at 10:13:06PM +0000, John Watts wrote:
> 
> MD5 (6.2-RELEASE-i386-disc1.iso) = 3d27214700687c0b5390e8b6dd3706e3 (your MD5SUM)
>                                    CBBD52B257FB1FDE3E22A13CC8EAAE85 (my calc)
> 
> The program I used to calculate the md5 of the downloaded iso comes up with
> a different md5 than that posted in the download directory, although it calculated the same MD5 for the other two files (disc2 and docs).  
> 
> My file manager reports the size of the disc1.iso is 601,229,312 bytes.  I downloaded the 6.2 release from the first in the list of the U.S ftp servers.  ie
> from here:
> 
> ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/
> >How-To-Repeat:
> recalculate the md5Sum for 6.2-Release-disc1.iso, compare result to your md5sum that you include with the release iso's.
> >Fix:
> 

Hello John,

      My official copy from one of the dutch ftp servers has the correct md5 sum

      -bash-2.05b$ md5 6.2-RELEASE-i386-disc1.iso
      MD5 (6.2-RELEASE-i386-disc1.iso) = 3d27214700687c0b5390e8b6dd3706e3

      The sizes are the same as on the official server, I am downloading that
      one now to see any potential changes.

      //Remko


-- 
Kind regards,

     Remko Lodder               ** remko@elvandar.org
     FreeBSD                    ** remko@FreeBSD.org

     /* Quis custodiet ipsos custodes */
